query_text: When is GTA 6 releasing now? And what is the story this time?
sources:
[{"content":"Mar 20, 2026 · GTA 6 is confirmed to release on November 19, 2026, after being delayed twice. The game’s first trailer debuted on December 4, 2023, officially revealing that it would arrive in 2025.


        # GTA 6 Release Date, Gameplay, Story, & Everything We Know (2026 Update) - https://fandomwire.com/gta-6-everything-we-know/
        After years of rumors and speculations, not to mention delays, GTA 6 is confirmed to launch later this year on current-generation consoles. The hype and anticipation around this title are unprecedented, and it’ll undoubtedly be the biggest video game release ever. So, to keep the excitement going, here are the details you need to know about GTA 6.



1 What do We Know About GTA 6’s Release Date?









GTA 6 is confirmed to release on November 19, 2026, after being delayed twice. The game’s first trailer debuted on December 4, 2023, officially revealing that it would arrive in 2025. However, on May 2, 2025, Rockstar announced on their newswire that the game was delayed to May 26, 2026, stating that they “need this extra time to deliver at the level of quality you expect and deserve.”



This was the first delay announcement. On May 6, 2025, Rockstar released the second trailer for GTA 6, reaffirming the May 26, 2026, release date. Their website was updated with new visuals and the release date. Yet, on November 6, 2025, Rockstar shared a post on X, announcing the second delay of GTA 6 to its current release date, November 19, 2026. 




Rockstar stated that “these extra months will allow us to finish the game with the level of polish you have come to expect and deserve” as a reason for the November 19 delay. While the delays were unfortunate, Rockstar has a history of delaying their titles multiple times, so this pattern was normal for the studio.





To better understand this, let’s use the example of GTA 5, which was delayed as well. On October 25, 2011, Rockstar officially announced the game in a tweet with the caption “#GTA5”. The first trailer for the game was released on November 2, 2011, but it did not provide a release date or window.



It wasn’t until October 30, 2012, that Rockstar confirmed on their newswire that GTA 5 would be launching in Spring 2013. Pre-orders opened on November 5, 2012, followed by the second trailer, which debuted on November 14. Trailer 2 further solidified the Spring 2013 release window.



However, on January 31, 2013, Rockstar confirmed on their newswire that GTA 5 will release on September 17, 2013, stating that the game “simply needs a little more polish to be of the standard we and, more importantly, you require”, similar to what happened with GTA 6. This date ended up being the final release date for GTA 5, with no further delays.



Now, for the PC version of GTA 5, it was slated to release with the PS4 and Xbox One versions of the game in Fall 2014, as confirmed in Rockstar’s newswire on June 9, 2014. A newswire post from Rockstar on September 12, 2014, from Rockstar confirmed November 18, 2014, as the release date for the next-gen console versions of GTA 5, but the PC version was delayed to January 27, 2015.



Rockstar announced a second delay for the PC version on January 13, 2015, setting the release date on March 24, 2015, and citing “a few extra weeks of testing and polish” as the reason. On February 24, 2015, Rockstar announced a third delay for the PC version to April 14, 2015, “to ensure that the game is as polished as possible”, and that ended up being the final release date for GTA 5 PC.



Comparing both games, GTA 5 was delayed once, while GTA 6 was delayed twice, which isn’t unprecedented, as the upcoming game will be significantly larger in every aspect than its predecessor. Will it get delayed again? Chances are low, as Take-Two’s latest earnings call confirmed that the game’s marketing will begin this summer.



As for things that haven’t been confirmed yet, Rockstar hasn’t officially said anything about GTA 6 PC or the release of a new GTA Online. GTA 5’s Online counterpart launched on October 13, 2013, weeks after the game’s initial release. However, Rockstar is yet to confirm a new GTA Online counterpart for GTA 6.




Recently, reports claimed that GTA 6’s title IDs were added to the PlayStation database, further fueling excitement and sparking speculation that pre-orders are set to begin. However, Rockstar has since removed the IDs from PlayStation’s database after fans found a glitch that made the game appear on their Recently Played list on PS5.



Interestingly, Rockstar Games is currently hiring Game Testers in Bangalore, as spotted by user bigfoot_manhunter on the GTA 6 subreddit, which could point towards an announcement in the near future. Among this and rumors suggesting that Trailer 3 will drop in April, take everything with a pinch of salt.



2 All Confirmed GTA 6 Platforms



Image Credit: Rockstar Games



GTA 6 is confirmed to launch only on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S on November 19, 2026. A PC version of the game has not been announced by Rockstar yet. As we mentioned above, the PC version of GTA 5 launched nearly two years after the game initially launched on PS3 and Xbox 360. So, you can expect a similar release timeframe for the PC version of GTA 6.



This console-first release formula for some of Rockstar’s biggest titles will continue with GTA 6. However, there are reasons why the studio takes more time to release their biggest hits on PC, as opposed to the console versions. Optimisations and anti-cheat make up some of the reasons. 



When GTA 5 was delayed for a third time on PC, Rockstar explained that the PC version of the game was their “most graphically and technically advanced version yet, fully optimized to support a broad range of current hardware and features a host of PC-specific enhancements including greater levels of detail, deep configuration options and ultra-high-definition support,” so it’s more than likely that the case is the same for GTA 6.




Another reason why Rockstar delays their PC releases is the mods, which is something that GTA 6 will definitely have, as the studio recently opened up its official mod storefront called Cfx Marketplace. Mods have always been a core part of the GTA experience on PC, and because Rockstar has launched its own mod marketplace, extensive mod support in GTA 6 is all but confirmed.



Returning to GTA 6’s platforms, the open world title will not be launching on previous generation consoles such as PlayStation 4 and Xbox One, due to technological limitations. Not only have game releases for past-gen consoles slowed down, but old-gen consoles also do not have the technological power to handle next-generation visual fidelity and mechanics, especially a game as detailed and massive as GTA 6.



So, GTA 6 will be released only on current-generation consoles (PS5 and Xbox Series X|S). Coming to the performance of Rockstar’s upcoming game, rumors that GTA 6 will run at 30 FPS on consoles have been circulating. Rockstar hasn’t officially confirmed performance targets, but based on GameGPU’s analysis, here’s what you can expect:



CONSOLEPERFORMANCE MODEQUALITY MODEBALANCED MODEPlayStation 5– 1080p Resolution (Upscaled to 1440p)– 60 FPS– Balanced Graphics Settings– 1440p Resolution (Upscaled to 4K)– 30 FPS– High SettingsNAPlayStation 5 Pro– 1080p Resolution (Upscaled to 1440p)– Upto 120 FPS– Reduced post-processing for better FPS– 2160p Resolution (4K)– 30 FPS-Max Graphics Settings (with RT)– 1440p Resolution (Upscaled to 4K)– 60 FPS– Balanced Graphics Settings (with compromises)Xbox Series X– 1080p Resolution (Upscaled to 1440p)– 60 FPS– Moderate Graphics Settings– 1440p Resolution (Upscaled to 4K)– 30 FPS– High Graphics SettingsNAXbox Series SNA– Dynamic 480p – 720p Resolution (Upscaled up to 1080p)– 30 FPS– Low to Medium Graphics Settings– No RTNA



These are all predictions, and performance targets have not been revealed by Rockstar. The game will be demanding even on current-generation consoles. However, Rockstar’s games have always been well-optimized, and the second trailer for GTA 6 was captured on a base PS5, running at smooth 30FPS with ray-traced reflections.



3 Everything We Know About GTA 6’s Story, Setting, & Characters



Image Credit: Rockstar Games



GTA 6 will take place in the state of Leonida in the present day (2020s), following the journey of dual protagonists and criminals Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos as they find themselves inside a criminal conspiracy after a bank robbery gone wrong, forcing them to depend on each other for survival.




Rockstar’s upcoming game is confirmed to feature the state of Leonida as the map, which includes locations such as Vice City, Grassrivers, Leonida Keys, Port Gellhorn, Ambrosia, and Mount Kalaga National Park. The romantic criminal duo, Jason and Lucia, are confirmed as the playable characters in the story.



While the confirmed locations include the ones mentioned above, a handful of landmarks have also been leaked/identified. Some of the identified landmarks and areas include Hamlet, Ekanfinaka, Red Hill Forest, Venetian Islands, South Beach, and more. It’s worth noting that these locations have not been confirmed and could be removed in the final game.



Supporting characters include Cal Hampton (Jason’s sceptical friend), Boobie Ike (controls a business empire in Vice City), Dre’Quen Priest (owner of Only Raw Records), Bae-Luxe and Roxy (signed to Only Raw Records under the name “Real Dimez”), Raul Bautista (veteran bank robber), and Brian Heder (Jason’s tenant and a seasoned drug runner).



GTA 6 has seen multiple leaks over the years, ranging from gameplay leaks to story and mission leaks. Take everything with a grain of salt, but the leak from June 2025, which suggested that the game has 700+ enterable shops, a six-star wanted level, unique character abilities, and more, got a Rockstar employee fired.



However, leaks such as Lucia’s central role in the story, Lucia abandoning her child, the limited weapon system, chapter divisions, the relationship bar, and more remain unconfirmed. What’s confirmed is that Jason has an Army background, and he worked for drugrunners in the Keys. As for Lucia, she was imprisoned at Leonida Penitentiary after fighting for her family, who are from Liberty City.




Jason and Lucia meet, they fall in love in a Bonnie and Clyde-inspired narrative, and they’re forced to stick to each other after a failed bank robbery. When it comes to the setting, GTA 6 evidently parodies the modern day, showcasing the social media and influencer culture, referencing popular memes and events that took place in Florida, as Leonida is based on the real state of Florida.



4 What Can We Expect From GTA 6’s Brand New Gameplay?



Image Credit: Rockstar Games



Rockstar hasn’t released dedicated gameplay footage from GTA 6, but specific snippets in the game’s trailers have given us an idea of what the gameplay will look like. It will be an evolution of GTA 5’s gameplay, directly building upon its foundation and expanding the mechanics in significant ways. You can expect typical GTA gameplay, but exponentially improved in every single way.



The visuals look much better than GTA 5; the fidelity has increased, thanks to next-generation hardware and technology, and the game overall looks more detailed and richer. From the trailers, explosions, particle effects, wildlife, hair physics, debris, NPC density, gym animations, everything looks more lively than GTA 5.



However, leaks suggest that Rockstar is taking cues from Red Dead Redemption 2’s gameplay for GTA 6. While Rockstar hasn’t said anything about this, the recent Mansions DLC in GTA Online was likely testing the waters for purchasable properties in GTA 6. Leaks also state that weight mechanics, enhanced gore, improved driving and GPS, dual-control character wheel, dead-eye-like abilities, and more will be present in GTA 6’s gameplay.



As for the open world, the state of Leonida will be much larger than GTA 5’s San Andreas map, filled with diverse areas and locations. The trailers also showcased dynamic events happening in the open world, such as police chases, raids, realistic NPC behaviour, weather changes, and dynamic time of day, among other elements.




Rockstar has confirmed that GTA 6 will be “the biggest and most immersive evolution of Grand Theft Auto yet”, so expect a host of gameplay changes, freedom in the open world, and new mechanics with added realism. Leaked development footage showed new mechanics, improved NPC AI, and other gameplay elements.



If you were worried about Rockstar using Generative AI to develop GTA 6, then put those worries to rest as Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick has confirmed that the world of GTA 6 is handcrafted by the developers, no Gen AI used. Additionally, the CEO also confirmed that Rockstar won’t follow a digital-only release for the game at launch, as rumors suggested.



In a recent interview with The Games Business, Zelnick doubled down on his stance on Generative AI in GTA 6, stating that, “the notion that somehow new tools would allow an individual to push a button and generate a hit and bring it to many millions of consumers around the world, it’s a laughable notion”. While Zelnick isn’t completely anti-AI, this is refreshing to hear, especially after the recent DLSS 5 fiasco.



Here’s a quick FAQ to jog your memory on GTA 6’s key details:




When is Grand Theft Auto 6 releasing?


GTA 6 will release on November 19, 2026. Marketing is confirmed to begin this summer.





Was GTA 6 delayed?
The game was delayed twice. The original release date was 2025, after which it was delayed to May 26, 2026, and then to November 19, 2026.


What platforms will GTA 6 be available on at launch?
GTA 6 will be available on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S at launch. It will not be coming to last-generation consoles.


Is GTA 6 coming to PC?
A PC port of GTA 6 has not been officially announced by Rockstar Games yet. However, judging from the studio’s previous releases, a PC version of the game will likely arrive sometime after its launch this year.


Is GTA 6 set in Vice City?
GTA 6 will be set in the state of Leonida (based on Florida), which includes Vice City as a location.


How many protagonists does GTA 6 have?


The game will feature two protagonists: Jason Duval and Lucia Caminos. Both of them will be playable.





How is GTA 6 gameplay different from GTA 5?
GTA 6 will be building on the foundation laid by GTA 5’s gameplay, introducing new mechanics and improving on every single aspect, from combat to exploration to driving.


Can the GTA 6 release date change again?
GTA 6’s release date has an extremely low chance of changing, as marketing will begin this summer and Take-Two is confident in the November 19 release date.




So, are you excited for Grand Theft Auto 6? What are you looking forward to the most? Share your thoughts and opinions in the comment section below.
